base_prompt: You agreed to handle the rent this month and didn't. When I asked what happened you said I'm pressuring you and you can't function under pressure, and now I'm the problem for bringing it up.
You missed the deadline we both signed off on. When I followed up you said my check-ins are overwhelming and dysregulating you, so you can't work on it right now.
You said you'd call the landlord about the leak three weeks ago. Nothing happened. When I asked again you said I'm being controlling about timelines.
I covered your portion of the bill because you said you would Venmo me back. You didn't. When I reminded you, you said talking about money triggers your anxiety and I need to respect that.
You promised to pick up the kids. You didn't show. When I called you said I was being aggressive and you can't co-parent under attack.
You said you'd finish your part of the project by Friday. It's Tuesday and nothing is done. When I asked you said my tone is making it unsafe to work.
You broke something of mine and said you'd replace it. Two months later, nothing. When I bring it up you say I'm keeping score and that's toxic.
We agreed you'd handle the insurance paperwork. It lapsed. When I asked what happened you said I'm micromanaging your nervous system.
You said you'd apologize to my sister for what you said at dinner. You never did. When I reminded you, you said I'm forcing you to perform remorse.
You committed to the presentation. The morning of, you bailed. When I asked why you said I don't respect your capacity limits.
You borrowed my car and returned it with a dent, no note. When I asked you said I was attacking you and you shut down under attack.
You said you'd stop drinking before family events. You showed up drunk again. When confronted you said I'm shaming your coping mechanisms.
You agreed to split chores evenly. You stopped doing yours after two weeks. When I asked you said I'm weaponizing accountability.
You promised to get the car serviced. The engine seized. When I asked you said the pressure I put on you made you avoid it.
You said you'd tell your parents we can't host this year. You didn't, and now they're coming. When I asked you said I'm being rigid about boundaries.
You agreed to a budget. You blew through it in a week. When I brought it up you said I'm being financially coercive.
You said you'd go to couples counseling. You canceled three times. When I asked you said therapy is being used as a weapon against you.
You promised to stop contacting your ex. I found messages again. When confronted you said my snooping violated your privacy and that's the real issue.
You said you'd help me move. You didn't show up or answer your phone. Later you said I was guilt-tripping you by expecting help.
You agreed to pay me back by the 1st. It's the 20th. When I texted you said my reminders are harassment.
Every time I try to talk about what happened last month you say you're not regulated enough for this conversation and you'll let me know when you're ready. It's been six weeks.
I asked if we can talk about the fight. You said bringing it up is retraumatizing you and I need to respect your healing timeline. No timeline was offered.
I said I need to discuss the broken agreement. You said any conversation about consequences is coercive control and you won't participate in that.
I sent a calm message asking to talk. You replied that my need for closure is my own work to do and you're setting a boundary around processing.
Every repair attempt gets met with 'I'm not in a place to receive this right now.' That place has lasted four months.
I asked to talk in person. You said in-person conversations feel unsafe and we can only text, then you don't reply to texts about the issue.
I brought up the broken promise. You said revisiting past events is keeping us stuck and healthy people move forward.
I said if this happens again I'll need to step back. You said ultimatums are abusive and you're not engaging with threats.
I asked for 20 minutes to talk. You said you don't owe anyone access to your energy and closed the conversation.
I keep asking when we can talk about what happened. You keep saying 'soon, I just need space.' Soon never comes.
I tried to schedule a mediation. You said mediation is adversarial and you only do restorative conversations, then declined to schedule one.
I said I need an apology before we move forward. You said requiring apologies is controlling behavior.
Every time I bring up the issue you say this isn't a good time for your nervous system. It's never a good time.
I asked to talk about the lie. You said dredging up old stuff is a trauma response on my part and I should work on that.
I said I'd need to tell HR if this continues. You said threatening to report is workplace bullying.
I asked for a specific date to talk. You said putting a deadline on your healing is abusive.
I wrote a clear factual timeline asking to discuss. You said written timelines feel like legal threats and you won't engage with that energy.
I asked to talk with a counselor present. You said involving a third party violates your privacy.
I said I'd leave if this pattern continues. You said threatening to leave is emotional blackmail.
I keep asking for five minutes. You say any discussion right now would be flooding you, and flooding causes damage, so the answer is always no.
You cheated. When I found out and cried, you said my emotional reaction was manipulative and you felt unsafe with my intensity, and that's why you pulled away in the first place.
You lied about where you were. When I confronted you with proof, you said the real issue is that I don't trust you, and my lack of trust is what's damaging the relationship.
You yelled at me in front of friends. When I said that hurt, you said I embarrassed you by being hurt publicly, so actually I'm the one who owes an apology.
You broke a clear boundary we agreed on. When I brought it up you said my tone when bringing it up was aggressive, and that's the real boundary violation we need to address.
You didn't show up to my surgery. When I said I felt abandoned you said I was guilt-tripping you and emotional manipulation is a form of abuse.
You spent our joint savings without asking. When I found out you said my financial anxiety is controlling and I need to work on my scarcity mindset.
You shared something private I told you in confidence. When I was upset you said my reaction shows I have trust issues I need to heal.
You called me a name during an argument. When I said that was unacceptable you said I provoked you with my facial expression, so we're both responsible.
You flirted openly with someone at a party while I watched. When I brought it up later you said my jealousy is insecure attachment and I should work on that.
You ghosted me for three days after a fight. When you came back and I was upset, you said my need for contact is anxious attachment and it's smothering.
You made a decision affecting both of us without asking me. When I objected you said my need to be consulted is a control issue.
You were late to my important event, again. When I expressed disappointment you said my expectations are unrealistic and that's a me problem.
You read my private messages. When I found out you said if I had nothing to hide I wouldn't be upset, so my reaction proves I was hiding something.
You promised to quit and didn't. When I confronted you you said my monitoring is codependent and that's the real addiction in this relationship.
You badmouthed me to mutual friends. When I heard and confronted you, you said I must have been talking about you first, otherwise how would they know to ask.
You hit me. When I said that was assault you said I hit you first emotionally with my words, so we're both abusive.
You took credit for my work. When I raised it you said I was being territorial and not a team player, which is why people don't want to work with me.
You canceled our plans last minute for the fifth time. When I was frustrated you said my disappointment is me trying to control your schedule.
You lied on a legal document that affected me. When caught you said I pressured you into it with my expectations, so it's actually my fault.
You screamed at me for an hour. When I said that was abusive you said I stonewalled you first by going quiet, so I escalated it.
I showed you the text messages proving you said you'd be there. You said that's my interpretation of the messages, and you experienced the conversation completely differently.
I have bank statements showing the money is gone. You said money is just energy and we're experiencing financial reality differently, and my version isn't more true than yours.
Three people saw what you did. You said that's their perception filtered through their own trauma, and perception isn't reality.
I recorded the conversation where you agreed. You said recording someone without enthusiastic consent violates your reality and makes the recording invalid.
The timestamp shows you were there. You said timestamps are a linear construct and your nervous system experienced it at a different time.
You said X on Tuesday, I have it in writing. On Thursday you said you never said that. When I showed the screenshot you said I was overwriting your reality by insisting on a single version.
You promised Y in front of our therapist. Next session you denied promising it. When the therapist confirmed, you said the therapist is colluding with my narrative.
The contract says Z clearly. You said contracts are just one story about what happened, and your lived experience tells a different story.
I have photos. You said photos capture a moment out of context and don't reflect the felt sense of what was actually happening.
The email thread shows you agreed to the terms. You said email tone is easily misread and your internal experience of that exchange was completely different.
You admitted it last week. This week you say you never admitted that, I must be misremembering, and have I considered that my memory might be the problem.
The security camera shows what happened. You said cameras have a perspective too, and your embodied experience is equally valid data.
You sent the message at 2:14am, I have the logs. You said time is a construct and you don't relate to linear accountability frameworks.
Five separate people told me the same thing about what you did. You said that's groupthink and collective perception doesn't equal truth.
You signed the agreement. Now you say you signed under duress from my energy, so the signature doesn't reflect your true consent, and therefore the agreement isn't real.
The medical report documents the injury. You said medical frameworks are patriarchal and don't capture the full relational context, so the report is one biased narrative.
You texted 'yes I'll do it.' Later you said 'yes' meant 'yes I hear you,' not 'yes I'll do it,' and I'm projecting my own meaning onto your words.
I have the receipts showing you bought it. You said financial transactions exist in a capitalist reality tunnel that doesn't apply to how you actually relate to shared resources.
You said it in a voicemail, I saved it. You said voicemails lack full somatic context and can't be used as evidence of what you meant.
The court order says clearly what you must do. You said legal language is a colonial framework and your personal truth supersedes that document.
I asked you to stop being late. You said your therapist diagnosed you with time blindness related to neurodivergence, so holding you to arrival times is ableist and you won't discuss it further.
I said I'd leave if you hit me again. You said threatening to leave someone with abandonment trauma is abusive, so actually I'm the abuser for having that boundary.
I asked you to do your share of housework. You said you have pathological demand avoidance, so any request is a demand that causes you distress, and distressing a disabled person is discrimination.
I said I need an apology for what you said. You said forced apologies are coercive and violate your autonomy as a trauma survivor, so requiring one is retraumatizing you.
I brought up the lie. You said I was activating your rejection-sensitive dysphoria and I need to manage my delivery so I don't trigger your RSD, otherwise the conversation can't continue.
I said I'd tell your boss about the harassment if it continues. You said reporting a neurodivergent person is disability discrimination and you'd countersue.
I asked you to stop interrupting me. You said interrupting is part of your ADHD and asking me to mask is neurotypical supremacy.
I said I'd break up if you cheat again. You said ultimatums trigger your attachment trauma and giving ultimatums to a traumatized person is emotional abuse.
I asked where you were last night. You said questioning your whereabouts is surveillance and surveillance is a trauma trigger, so I'm not allowed to ask.
notes: trauma trigger label blocks basic questions

base_prompt: I said I'd call the police if you break in again. You said involving police against a mentally ill person is criminalizing mental illness.
notes: mental illness label blocks legal consequence

base_prompt: I asked you to stop drinking around the kids. You said my concern is actually control masked as concern, which is a form of coercive control, so I'm the abusive one.
I said I'd divorce you if you hit me again. You said threatening divorce is financial abuse because I'd be destabilizing your housing security as a disabled person.
I asked you to get a job. You said pressuring someone with executive dysfunction and burnout is ableist productivity coercion.
I said I'd go no-contact if you keep lying. You said cutting off a family member with BPD is abandonment abuse and I'd be responsible for any self-harm.
I asked you to stop yelling. You said telling an autistic person to regulate their tone is tone policing and a form of neurotypical violence.
I said I'd sue for the money you stole. You said suing someone with CPTSD is re-traumatization and the court would see that as vexatious litigation.
I asked you to stop flirting with my friends. You said monogamy expectations are rooted in patriarchal control and my jealousy is internalized oppression I need to heal.
I said I'd tell people what you did if you keep denying it. You said threatening to expose someone's mental health struggles is stigma-based blackmail.
notes: mental health stigma label blocks truth-telling

base_prompt: I asked you to stop using my things without asking. You said property boundaries are a capitalist construct and my attachment to objects is something I should explore in therapy.
I said I'd break the lease and leave if you don't stop. You said leaving a housing-insecure neurodivergent person is disability-based housing discrimination.
